project.name: research_x
project.one_liner: A local, evidence-first memory and search system for an existing X collection database.
project.purpose: Let an AI agent query local X collection data while preserving provenance, account-specific bookmark ownership, quote and media context, and the user's subjective interests.
project.scope: Local repository facts for the first presentation deck; generated decks, diagrams, WBS, pointer maps, and ChatGPT captures are excluded as evidence.

node.purpose: Presentation-only D2 and Marp build boundary.

tech_stack.name: Python package
tech_stack.role: Primary local application and CLI implementation.

tech_stack.name: SQLite
tech_stack.role: Local storage for acquisition data, memory documents, workflow traces, evals, and provider budget guard state.

tech_stack.name: D2 and Marp
tech_stack.role: Presentation diagram and deck rendering lane only.

modules.name: memory
modules.purpose: Searchable document projections, evidence restoration, context bundles, answers, workflows, evals, and audit surfaces.

modules.name: presentation
modules.purpose: Local validation boundary for facts-first presentation generation.

boundaries.name: Evidence invariant
boundaries.rule: Raw sources, searchable documents, search results, source bundles, context chunks, citations, and answers are separate objects.

boundaries.name: No-quota provider freeze
boundaries.rule: Real provider calls remain blocked unless explicitly lifted in the current conversation and budget preflight passes.

boundaries.name: Presentation facts boundary
boundaries.rule: Slide-worthy claims must come from repository-file evidence and must not use generated or control artifacts as evidence.

data_stores.name: X collection SQLite database
data_stores.purpose: Stores raw tweets, bookmarks, accounts, media, edges, and collection state.

data_stores.name: Memory schema tables
data_stores.purpose: Stores rebuildable memory documents, embeddings, evals, workflows, governance, and search traces.

external_dependencies.name: Provider-backed memory and answer lanes
external_dependencies.gate: No-quota provider freeze and API Budget Guard.

external_dependencies.name: D2 and Marp Node dependencies
external_dependencies.gate: Presentation build surface only; no plugins, hooks, MCP, or provider calls.

key_flows.name: Acquisition to local store
steps: Run an adapter through the resilient acquisition pipeline.
Normalize results into the local X store.
Use local database rows as the raw provenance base.

key_flows.name: Memory workflow
steps: Build or query searchable memory documents.
Restore source bundles before claims are supported.
Produce context chunks, citation annotations, answer status, and workflow traces.

key_flows.name: Presentation generation
steps: Render D2 diagrams from validated facts.
Build Marp slides only after facts and slides exist.

tables.name: memory_documents
tables.purpose: Rebuildable searchable projections over raw X source rows.

tables.name: memory_document_fts
tables.purpose: SQLite FTS index over searchable document text.

tables.name: memory_workflow_runs
tables.purpose: Stored workflow traces for route choice, evidence state, context, and answer output.

tables.name: memory_api_usage_events
tables.purpose: Budget-guard usage records for provider-facing operations.

claims.claim: research_x is a local, user-specific X data memory/search project whose goal is AI-callable search with provenance preserved.
The memory pipeline is evidence-first: raw sources, searchable documents, results, bundles, chunks, citations, and answers are separate stages.
Search hits are candidates and cannot support claims until they restore a source bundle and pass citation checks.
Provider-backed embeddings, OCR, rerank, Reader, classifier, answer, external search, and managed-RAG lanes are blocked by the no-quota freeze until explicitly approved.
The primary Python command surface is the research-x CLI implemented by research_x.cli:main.
The local memory layer stores searchable documents, FTS rows, workflow traces, eval records, embeddings, relations, governance records, and API budget events in SQLite tables.
The presentation lane uses a local D2/Marp build boundary, but factual deck content must first pass the PresentationFacts validator.
Generated decks, diagram assets, WBS state, pointer maps, and consultation captures are control or presentation artifacts, not evidence or answer support.

unknowns.question: Whether editable PowerPoint objects are required instead of fixed-layout Marp output.
unknowns.reason: The current approved lane only establishes D2/Marp fixed-layout generation; PptxGenJS and Marp editable PPTX remain deferred.

unknowns.question: The final slide order and diagram count for the first deck.
unknowns.reason: Slides must be derived after facts validation and diagram requirements are reviewed.
